a:r.a:t: Telemarketing Representative | External Compensation Plan Assessment
HGV uses a wide variety of metrics within plans (1-5) where market plans are more narrowly focused
bookings / package sales, show-ups, and downstream purchase outcomes.
HGV utilizes a more complex structure (5–8 tiers) vs. the market standard of 3–5 tiers, potentially reducing clarity and line-of-sight for reps.
Total Compensation Range (i.e., Leverage / Actual potential earnings)
Net Package Sales; Conversion %; Dept Destination; Team APP; Tours Booked; Number of Arrivals; Show; VPG
Downstream purchase outcomes on booked tours
1-8 tiered levels with progressively higher payouts as performance increases across tiers
3-5 tiered levels with progressively higher payouts as performance increases across tiers
Weekly / Bi-weekly & Monthly components (with occasional quarterly true-ups)
SPIFFs: Drive immediate behavior changes (same-day/over few days)
Budgets are small and typically a fixed dollar amount
STIs: Run for longer durations (up to ~3 months) to encourage behavior not built into the plan
Directors/VPs control SPIF/STI budgets but approval is required prior to launch
Monetary: Ad hoc SPIFFs used by managers to provide additional incentives for priority tours, off-season periods, and short-term performance pushes
Non-monetary: Contests, prizes, time-off perks, and experiential rewards used to boost engagement and retention
Consolidated review of all telemarking representative roles including call center, inbound agents, outbound agents, etc.
*Represents actual earnings data from 1/1/2025 through 12/31/2025, however does not represent mid-year job changes, and therefore might not be accurate for every employee
Where HGV Varies from the Market
